dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (lah as confident-affirmation)
notes: Sentence-final 'lah' on a capability assertion ('pasti bisa' = 'definitely can / surely able') doubles down on the confidence — speaker is reassuring or pep-talking. English 'obviously' captures the no-doubt register. Indonesian 'pasti bisa' is often subject-elided (could be 'I can', 'you can', 'we can' depending on context); the fixture interprets it as 'we can' for the encouragement use-case. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'of course' might be warmer; 'no doubt' more emphatic; the subject choice (we/you/I) depends on who's being encouraged.
